title: Eldridge Dedicated Capacity
preview: Customer building a customer support assistant and internal knowledge search for financial products; needs low-latency interactive chat for customers plus high-throughput embedding/reranking for search. Sensitive to cost but prioritizes tail latency predictability during market events.
text: Customer building a customer support assistant and internal knowledge search for financial products; needs low-latency interactive chat for customers plus high-throughput embedding/reranking for search. Sensitive to cost but prioritizes tail latency predictability during market events.

title: Hummingbird Wellness Studio
preview:
Inbound via self-serve signup (trial); founder Clara Patel reached out after playing with API keys. Team = 12 (8 admin/clinicians + 4 eng/ops). Primary goal: deploy a front-end triage chat and summarize intake forms into EHR fields.

Conversation highlights:
- "Founder: 'Need quick triage to reduce admin hours, must avoid storing PHI longer than necessary.'"
- They are NOT asking for Private/VPC yet — want hosted API on public cloud but with strong retention controls and audit logs.
- Concerned about HIPAA-adjacent wording: they will not store PHI intentionally but intake sometimes includes identifiers; ask for redaction options and opt-out of persistent logging.
- Latency needs: acceptable to 200-500ms per response; throughput low (peak ~20 concurrent users). Cost-sensitive — early stage.
- Model preferences: prefer open-base models or small Llama2-70B quantized variants; also open to Redwood-curated verified models if latency/cost look good.

Requested artifacts (from call): security FAQ, hosted retention policy, sample API config to disable long-term logs, SOC2 summary, KMS integration notes. CEO requested link to billing tiers for small teams.

Quotes/phrases: 'We want the API to feel like a swiss-army knife for our intake flow' — Clara.

Lead quality: warm inbound, trial active, engineering will test with dev key this week. Will escalate to procurement only if retention/controls confirmed.

Short-term plan: share retention controls doc + sample curl to enable ephemeral mode; follow-up security call to answer HIPAA-adjacent Qs.

Sales notes (shorthand): trial created 2026-02-10; sent quickstart 2026-02-11; demo scheduled 2026-02-12 (ff_20260212_01), awaiting security docs sent 2026-02-20.

title: Lithic Vector Mesh
preview:
Account overview: Lithic Vector Mesh is a large commerce platform (catalog + user signals) evaluating a move from in-house FAISS clusters to a managed inference+indexing platform. Primary focus: embeddings + reranking for product search and personalization at massive scale. Target index size in first phase: 100M vectors, year-2 target 600M-1B vectors.

Requirements summary:
- Throughput: sustained 300-500 qps for product-search queries, 1200 qps batch offline reranks during catalog refresh windows.
- Latency: P50 < 40ms, P95 < 150ms for online search + rerank path (goal: ~50ms end-to-end).
- Cost sensitivity: target unit cost per online query (embedding + ANN + rerank) < $0.0005; must show predictable per-token and per-query economics.
- Model preferences: open/quantized embedding models (in-house fine-tuned on product metadata) + candidate cross-encoder for reranking (can be hosted or run as Dedicated). Preference for Redwood to support mixed routing (cheap ANN + heavier cross-encoder fallback).
- Indexing: bulk ingest of 50M items/day during initial migration window; need incremental delta ingestion with idempotent upserts. Storage + shard management must support >100M vectors with efficient compaction.
- Caching/batching: aggressive prefix/KV caching and continuous batching required to hit cost/latency targets. Seeking Redwood Optimize recommendations for batching windows and cache TTLs.
- Deployment & compliance: VPC-hosted control plane, key-managed encryption (BYOK/HSM desirable), audit logs retained 2 years, US data residency for PII. SOC2 compliance required; ISO27001 a plus.

Recent timeline (CRM):
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (Ava, Derek) — overview of Redwood Dedicated + scale profile. Ref: ff_20251112_2210.
- 2025-12-03: Security questionnaire submitted (lithicvm -> redwood). Drive: security-questions-2026.xlsx.
- 2026-01-12: Deep-architecture session (infra + search eng). Discussed sharding, offline rerank cadence, and cost targets. Ref: ff_20260112_3890.
- 2026-01-30: Sent sample dataset (10M vectors) and ingestion spec; SE to validate mapping. gthread_2025-11-21_prod-search.
- 2026-02-18: Pricing & SLA review; legal raised encryption wording. Ref: ff_20260218_4412 and SEC-2710.

POC plan (proposed):
1) 4-week Dedicated POC ingesting 100M vectors (subset of catalog + user sessions), validate ingestion throughput and storage compaction.
2) Benchmark: run 100 qps steady-state + peak 500 qps for 1 hour; measure p50/p95 latency and unit cost.
3) Rerank integration: deploy cross-encoder on dedicated nodes as fallback; measure accuracy lift vs cost.
4) Deliver cost model: per-query breakdown (ANN + embedding + rerank + infra amortization).

Quotes / direct notes from stakeholders:
- "We need to be able to predict dollars-per-query before we push this into prod." — Lead Search Eng (M. Patel).
- "Procurement will not approve without explicit BYOK and audit retention language." — Security Lead (T. Rowe).

Next actions: Derek to share ingest playbook + estimated infra footprint; Ava to coordinate legal SOW revision; schedule POC kickoff with infra/infraops and security (target week of 2026-03-10).

Internal sales notes: strong technical buy-in (search eng + infra). Procurement and legal are main gating factors. Competitive runway includes Pinecone proof-of-concept and internal FAISS clusters; we must demonstrate unit cost and SLA advantages.

Attachments: redwood-pricing-poc-2026.pdf, security-questions-2026.xlsx, ticket INFR-4123 (ingest schema).

title: Emberfield Support Co.
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog post (ticket-summarization + tool-calling). Signed up self-serve trial last week — used Hosted API free credits.

Initial discovery call (2026-02-12) with CEO (Lina Park) + Eng Lead (Omar Ruiz). Quick notes:
- Use-case: automated ticket summarization + structured extraction (priority, product, root_cause) to pre-fill ticket fields and drive triage rules. Also want tool-calling: ability to trigger ticket actions (assign, escalate, add tag) from model output.
- Workload: mostly short tickets (50-300 tokens), 3k–8k msgs/day now, expect 20–30k in 6mo. Latency target: median < 150ms for single-turn summary, 95th < 400ms acceptable. Throughput: bursty (morning spikes). Cost sensitivity: high — SMB budget, wants guidance on batching and prefix caching.
- Model preferences: prefers community/open models; ok with quantized variants. Wants Redwood-hosted variants only (no Private).
- Routing/fallback: asked about automatic fallback to lower-cost model under load. Interested in canary + A/B for evaluation.
- Security: SOC2 required for vendor, SSO (SAML) for admin portal, audit logs retention min 90d, basic KMS support requested. No air-gapped/on-prem plans.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-02-10: self-serve signup, trial org created.
- 2026-02-12: discovery call (Fireflies ff-5a8c7d9b).
- 2026-02-15: trial usage spiked after running internal batching test (observed high per-token spend).
- 2026-02-18: emailed pricing question thread-u8Y2kzX2 asking about token-level discounts for summaries.
- 2026-02-19: SE intro scheduled; requested POC checklist and sample schema for structured output.

Quotes/snippets: "We need crisp, one-sentence summaries for agents + reliable entity extraction to map to our ticket fields." — Lina P.
"If we can keep cost predictable and show clear latency numbers, we can move to paid in a quarter." — Omar R.

POC ask (customer):
- 2-week POC using Hosted API: ingest 5k historical tickets, run summarization pipeline, return structured JSON with fields (summary, priority, product, root_cause, suggested_action).
- Demonstrate tool-calling flow: model suggests "escalate->tier2" and Redwood triggers webhook to create internal action. Need sample webhook flow.

Redwood recommended approach (internal notes):
- Provide quickstart repo + prebuilt prompt templates for ticket summarization and structured output.
- Example config for batching + KV cache to reduce cost on multi-turn agent followups.
- Share median/95th P99 latency benchmarks for similar SMB workloads.
- Offer 30m deep-dive demo with Marcus (SE) and Lina/Omar.

Next steps (short): send quickstart + sample schema by 2026-02-20; confirm demo slot; prepare small script to convert 1k historical tickets and run sample summarization with hosted_api credits.

Internal owner notes: Lina is pragmatic, budget-conscious. Prioritized speed of onboarding > advanced model tuning — emphasize turnkey templates and clear economics.

Stitch links: pricing deck draft in Drive, small snippet of Fireflies transcript attached to ticket INF-3421.

title: LanternLoop Diagnostics
preview:
- Latency target: 95th percentile < 200ms per token for 32-token responses (prefers <150ms).
- Throughput: bursts to ~200 concurrent short chats; baseline 20–40 rps.
- Cost sensitivity: startup budget; goal <= current OpenAI spend (founder wants numbers).
- Models: open to open models if quality matches; currently using gpt-4o-lite as baseline.
- Routing/fallbacks: want simple fallback to cheaper model on heavy load.
- Compliance: SOC2 mandatory; SSO (SAML) during prod onboarding; audit logs retained 90 days.

title: ClarityMesh
preview:
Background: ClarityMesh is a mid-market SaaS vendor that powers searchable knowledge bases for B2B SaaS customers (support docs, onboarding guides, internal KB). They operate multi-tenant SaaS with a high read volume on KB search and expect peak QPS for search reranking up to ~500 qps across regions.

Primary ask: Hosted embeddings + reranking pipeline. Need low-latency top-K retrieval and a tuned reranker to push relevant KB passages to the assistant. Looking for predictable per-query economics at scale — current internal run-rate on their homegrown vector stack is getting expensive in CPU/GPU ops, want to offload to Redwood if cost/latency wins.

Indexing profile: ~12M total passages today (regularly deduped), incremental ingest cadence: hourly deltas (~10k docs/hr), retention for support docs 3+ years. Currently use 1536-d embeddings, exploring 1024 quantized models for cost. Want support for near-real-time partial re-indexing and soft deletes.

Performance targets (customer stated):
- 95th pctile end-to-end query latency (retrieve + rerank) < 200ms for single-region queries (NA) under steady traffic.
- Peak throughput: sustained 500 qps, burst 1k qps for 2–5 min windows.
- Cost target: effective per-query cost (embeddings+rerank+storage) <= $0.0007 for baseline workload.

Model & routing preferences: Prefer open models for rerank (fine-tuned lightweight re-ranker) and lower-cost embedding models for vector index. Wants automatic fallback to cheaper model variant on capacity events; route read-heavy traffic to cached responses and tiered model pools.

Security/compliance: Must support SSO (SAML), audit logs for admin actions, KMS integration for key management, and EU data residency for a segment of customers. SOC2 Type II is a firm blocker toward procurement.

POC plan discussed: 2-week hosted API POC. Scope: replicate current traffic profile with synthetic replay (daily cadence + bursts), onboard a subset of 2M docs, run A/B against current in-house stack for latency/cost and relevance metrics (MRR@10, NDCG). Success criteria: <=200ms p95, relevance >= baseline, per-query cost meets target.

Recent activity timeline (CRM notes):
- 2026-01-20: Initial inbound via pricing form; AE assigned (Ava).
- 2026-01-22: Intro call (30m) — product fit confirmed, asked for hosted API latency numbers. (ff_20260122_introcall)
- 2026-01-28: Deep dive with SE (Marco) on indexing pattern and reranker architecture; shared sample dataset and synthetic QPS profile. Fireflies: ff_20260128_clarity-poc-call.
- 2026-02-03: Sent draft POC proposal + pricing assumptions (drive link). Waiting on legal/compliance to provide SOC2 evidence.
- 2026-02-10: Follow-up demo of streaming embeddings ingestion; discussed caching + KV prefix reuse for users with repeated queries.

Sales notes / quotes from calls: "We need a provider that behaves like a CDN for our knowledge—fast, cheap, and predictable." — Head of Search.

Technical notes / SE observations: Reranker latency is the critical path; prefer running reranker on CPU-optimized instance or small GPU that supports batched scoring. Embedding compute dominates cost at high QPS unless caching or dimensionality reduction applied. Recommend Redwood Optimize suggestions: 1) 1024-d quantized embeddings + productized reranker model, 2) aggressive prefix caching for sessionized users, 3) dynamic routing to cheaper model pool during non-peak windows.

Next steps (detailed):
- Legal to confirm SOC2 docs by 2026-02-18 (blocked).
- SE to provision hosted API POC environment + sample index (Marco) — ETA 2 business days after SOC2 confirmation.
- Run synthetic 72-hr stress replay with 500 qps steady and 1k qps bursts during POC; collect p95 latencies and token costs.
- Deliver POC report with cost projection and recommended config (batching, cache TTLs, quantization profile).

Short term ask from AE: "If we can show p95 <200ms and cost delta >= 25% vs their infra, we convert to paid Dedicated in trial 6–9 months."

title: Ironwood Sentry AI
preview:
Snapshot from AE/SE interactions and security review. POC never started — fell apart during security and resourcing alignment. Key points:
- 2025-11-04 inbound lead via trade show; initial discovery call (Ava Chen + Diego). Company running camera analytics across 12 facilities, wants near-real-time event classification and operator summarization.
- They insist on private deployment inside their VPC or fully on-prem for 3 'critical' sites. KMS control mandatory; FIPS and export considerations flagged.
- Security sent initial questionnaire (see linked doc). They flagged: no third-party KMS allowed unless HSM integration; 3-year retention + audit export requirements.
- SE calls 2025-12-03 demo + architecture walkthrough. Ironwood liked runtime performance claims but asked for proof of KMS/HSM integration and proof of audit log retention.
- Procurement then announced a company-wide cap on external SaaS spend (2026-01). POC budget deferred.
- Security lead (Marisol Vega) resigned in Jan 2026 during internal reorg; no replacement named. Internal champion evaporated.
- Competitor (local systems integrator) promised an on-prem appliance with delegated KMS and a short pilot on their pre-approved partner contract; procurement gravitated there due to pre-existing vendor relationship.
- AE notes: "They were the most security-sensitive prospect we've seen in this vertical — wanted explicit HSM certs and an onsite install option."
- POC status: never kicked off. Could not agree on success criteria (what counts as usable classification at 95%+ across camera types) and who funds engineering time.
- Current disposition: stalled/closed-lost (soft). Sales recommended leaving in nurture w/ quarterly check-ins; security FAQ updates to be shared if we add HSM integration details.

title: Mariner Insights Platform
preview:
Account background:
- Customer since 2023 (started on hosted API for embeddings + reranking). Started POC for Dedicated in Q4-2024 based on seasonal throughput needs. Renewal for hosted contract due 2025-06 but want to fold into Dedicated reserved capacity SOW.

Primary drivers:
- Predictable throughput for peak chartering season (May-Oct).
- Strong need for regional residency (APAC) for some customers and partners.
- Cost predictability: looking to move from per-token hosted spend to committed Dedicated with Optimize to reduce unit costs.

Technical requirements / targets (from SE calls):
- Workload: real-time dispatch suggestions (chat-like), bulk document embeddings/search, post-voyage summarization (batch).
- Latency target: p95 < 300ms for short-turn dispatch queries; tail latency most important.
- Throughput: sustained ~120–250 RPS at peak across multimodel routes.
- Model preferences: open-models (Llama2-13B quantized) validated in POC; fallbacks to smaller 7B variants for cost-sensitive routes.
- Routing: need policy-based routing (latency tier + cost tier) and automatic fallback to compatible quantized variants when Dedicated pool saturated.
- Security: SSO/SAML, KMS-managed key rotation, SOC2 + ISO27001 required, full audit logs and 1yr+ retention for access logs, APAC residency for subset of customer data.

POC & benchmarks (recent):
- Jan 2025 POC run against quantized 13B on g4dn-like nodes. Results: avg latency 170ms for short prompts; p95 measured 410ms — flagged by SE.
- Optimize pilot (two-week) reduced token spend on reranking pipeline by ~28% using autosuggestion batching + prefix caching. They liked caching behavior.
- Canary run with Dedicated staging pool (Feb 2025) saw improved stability but observed p95 spikes when KV cache warmup occurred. SE investigating warmup heuristics.

Engagement timeline (highlights):
- 2024-11-07: Intro + renewal timeline (hosted contract) — owner Ava.
- 2024-12-12: SE workshop (Diego) — architecture review, discussed VPC and on-prem options.
- 2025-01-09: Fireflies ff_20250109_4421 — POC kickoff, data residency scoping.
- 2025-01-28: Security deep-dive — provided SOC2 artifacts; asked for ISO27001 attestation.
- 2025-02-12: POC results review (ff_20250212_7938) — Optimize savings & latency gap noted.
- 2025-02-20: Pricing review with finance + CSM (Nina) — redlines on SOW.

Quotes & call snippets:
- \"We need predictable throughput for May — outages there cost us charters.\" — Head of Ops.
- \"If p95 doesn't hit the target, we can't commit to the higher ARR band.\" — Procurement lead.

Risks / blockers (details):
- APAC region pricing model still being finalized; affects cost-to-serve calculation for multi-region SOW.
- Legal needs explicit data-residency clause for Singapore and Japan customers — currently flagged by legal team.
- Benchmark discrepancy: POC p95 > target (410ms vs target 300ms). SE action: run NVMe-backed cache config + kernel selection tuning.

Commercials & next steps:
- They prefer a quarterly ramp for Dedicated capacity: 50% commit first 3 months, full commit ahead of peak season. Want Optimize adoption as mandatory add-on to realize cost savings.
- Next step: finalize Dedicated machine types + autoscaling policy (Diego to provide config by 2025-03-05).
- Legal: Redwood legal to supply APAC addendum draft by 2025-03-10. Procurement to sign SOW once p95 tuning and APAC pricing confirmed.
- CSM (Nina) to run 30-day Optimize onboarding workshop post-sign.

Quick notes (CRM shorthand):
- Pricing deck v2 shared (drive link).
- Procurement asked for EOR clause; redlines in SOW (INF-3421).
- Security: sent KMS integration checklist; awaiting signed NDA for on-prem docs.
- Happy with Optimize pilot savings; major win for commercial.

Attachments / refs: drive://redwood/pricing/mariner-dedicated-quote-v2.pdf, drive://redwood/security/mariner-security-answers-2025.docx, drive://redwood/pocs/mariner-poc-results-jan2025.xlsx

title: HarborLynx Platforms
preview:
Inbound from product org. Pilot on Hosted API completed 2/05 — optimistic on latency, mixed on cost. Product lead (CTO Marco Silva) said: "Hosted latency is fine for user flows but cost/unit is high at 1M tokens/mo scale." Wants move-to-Dedicated analysis. Short bullets: 
- P0: in-app chat + document search in single service
- P1: reranking search results for commerce site
- Security: must support company KMS + SAML SSO
- Data residency: US only; some EU later
- Fallback policy desired: hosted -> dedicated -> smaller-quantized on heavy load
